common: A Combination of Sulphonated Hydrocarbons, a Fossil Product of Complex Structure found in Tyrol, supposed to be Fish Deposits, contains 10 % Sulphur.

mental_emotional.text: Irritable and depressed. Forgetful, lack of concentration

head.text: Dull, aching; better cold, pressure. Dull frontal and supra-orbital headache; worse moving eyes, cold air; better, warmth

face.text: Skin feels dry and itches. Acne on chin

mouth_throat.text: Irritated; pain to ears; sore, dry, with hawking and expectoration

eyes.text: Burn, red; worse, any change of temperature

nose.text: Bland coryza; stuffed feeling; feels sore inside. Irresistible desire to sneeze

stomach_abdomen.text: Disagreeable taste, burning sensation, very thirsty. Nausea. Increased appetite
Disposition to soft, shapeless stools. Griping in umbilical and left hypogastric region. Early morning diarrhœa

urinary.text: Increased in quantity and frequency. Burning pain in meatus. Uric acid deposits

female.text: Fullness in lower abdomen. Nausea at time of menses

respiratory.text: Coryza; dry, teasing cough. Bronchiectasis and phthisis. Bronchitis, especially of the aged

skin.text: Heat and irritation; itching. Scaly and itching eczema. Crops of boils. Pruritus of pregnancy. Psorisis, Acne, rosacea, erysipelas

extremities.text: Lameness in right shoulder and right lower extremity

dosing.notes: Lower potencies. Externally, it is used as an ointment, with Lanoline 20 to 50 per cent; for chronic eczema and psoriasis, also acne rosacea and gouty joints. Chilblains, scabies. Rectal suppositories for senile prostate

keynotes.text: color="#800000">ICHTHYOLUM A Combination of Sulphonated Hydrocarbons, a Fossil Product of Complex Structure found in Tyrol, supposed to be Fish Deposits, contains 10 % Sulphur. Its action on skin, mucous membranes, and kidneys is prompt and useful. It is strongly antiparasitic; redness, pain and inflammation; decreases tension. Excellent in winter coughs of old people. Polyarthritis. Chronic rheumatism. Uric acid diathesis. Hay-fever. Chronic hives. Tuberculosis, aids nutrition. Alcoholism when nothing will stay on stomach.

This is an unproved remedy which has been used in old-school therapeutics as an external application, and internally in skin affections, rheumatism, scrofula, nephritis, and gonorrhœa. It is a powerful antiseptic. As an external application to rheumatic joints it has achieved marked results. A solution of equal parts of Ichthyol and water is warmed and applied to the affected joint on calico and covered with heated cloths, outside of which is an impervious fabric to prevent evaporation. Pure Ichthyol or an ointment of Lanoline and Ichthyol maybe used instead. Gouty joints have also been benefited. Ichthyol appears to act by virtue of its power of allaying pain and inflammation, but there can be no doubt that a thorough proving would bring out a specific relationship to the maladies in which it has been used with success. Scarpa claims to have used it with excellent effect in a large number of cases of phthisis.

generalities.text: Clinical: Arthritis; Gonorrhœa; Neuralgia; Phthisis; Pruritus vulvæ; Rheumatism; Skin, affections of

_raw: Ichth. belongs to the Petroleum class of remedies. Compare also Calc. caust. (lime-water compresses to allay inflammation); Sulph. Plantago (anodyne application); Carbol. ac. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
